message:
fix(sync): prevent canceled waiter accumulation in OnceCell

Add Drop impl for WaitInit that eagerly removes canceled waiter
registrations from the waiters list. Without this, repeated
cancel/drop cycles (e.g. a select! loop where the losing branch
is a get_or_init call) would accumulate stale waiter entries in
the Mutex-protected Vec until the initializer completes and calls
wake_all(), leading to unbounded memory growth proportional to
the number of cancellations rather than the number of live waiters.

The fix:
- On drop, marks the waiter's queued flag as false (Release ordering)
- Locks the waiters list and retains only entries whose Arc<AtomicBool>
  is not pointer-equal to the dropped waiter's Arc
- Handles poisoned mutex gracefully via into_inner()

Also adds a regression test that creates 128 cancel/drop cycles while
the cell is stuck in INITIALIZING and asserts the waiter list drains
to zero after all futures are dropped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
